criteria: is space something to be contested (is there conflict)
criteria_short: is space something to be contested
answer_options: Low contestation (minor competition, little open conflict)
High contestation (frequent conflict or strategic struggle)

criteria: can you inhabit and hold territory in space
criteria_short: can you inhabit and hold territory in space
answer_options: Visiting / Temporary presence only (stations, missions, outposts)
Limited settlement (small colonies, hard to sustain/control)
Habitable and governable (permanent settlements can hold territory)
Extensive territorial occupation (many settled worlds/regions held like states or empires)

criteria: how long is journey / how  far from earth
criteria_short: how long is journey / how  far from earth
answer_options: Long-distance journey (years, major separation from Earth)
Extreme / effectively unreachable (generational, completely separate, or one-way-feeling distance)

criteria: how culturally different is space from earth? (is it basically earth society but in space? or totally different/unique culture?)
criteria_short: how culturally different is space from earth?

criteria: do you speak same language on earth vs space (how diff / foreign is it) (is there a different language that everyone speaks, a universal translator? do they speak english)
criteria_short: do you speak same language on earth vs space ?
answer_options: Shared lingua franca plus local variation
Translation-mediated communication (universal translator / communication tech makes language difference irrelevant)

criteria: is it hostile (is it hard to survive out there? Not because of other actors, but just because of conditions…e.g, the Martian space is hostile, but there’s no one trying to kill Matt Damon)
criteria_short: is it hostile / hard to survive (bc of natural conditions)
answer_options: Nearly uninhabitable / lethal without major intervention

criteria: how common is living in space (e.g. is it 1 person journeying to space, or does everyone live in space)
criteria_short: how common is it for humans to live in space
answer_options: Normalized / widespread (space habitation is ordinary for humanity)

criteria: are there different countries in space? Or just one?
criteria_short: are there different countries in space? Or just one?
answer_options: Highly fragmented political landscape (multipolar with many factions, corporations, colonies, microstates)

criteria: genre of the book (e.g, horror, drama, comedy, fantasy…)
is space a military or civilian domain / is the book military or civilian?
criteria_short: is space a military or civilian domain / is the book military or civilian?
answer_options: Mostly civilian with some military presence

criteria: Is space characterized like another domain? (e.g ocean/navies with leaders as admirals, etc.; air/ airforces; or totally unique)
criteria_short: Is space characterized like another military domain?
answer_options: Like cyberspace / networked or abstract domain

criteria: how physically different is space from earth?
criteria_short: how physically different is space from earth?
answer_options: Radically non-Earth-like (physics/environment fundamentally unlike Earth experience)
